title: P0300 Random Misfire — Diagnostic Playbook
description: Decision tree for P0300 (random/multiple cylinder misfire). FLASHING MIL = stop driving immediately — catalyst overtemp risk. Covers spark, fuel, compression, and coil isolation.
physical_solution: P0300 = multiple cylinders misfiring randomly. Diagnose by eliminating: spark (swap coils), fuel (injector contribution), compression (cylinder-specific). P0300 + specific P030X = start with that cylinder.

uds_services: Mode $01 PID $06/$07 (fuel trims for lean misfire)
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Full — Mode $01 RPM and fuel trims accessible. Misfire per-cylinder counts via PID $4C-$53 on most 2010+ vehicles via ELM327. Freeze frame fully accessible.
preconditions: ⚠️ If MIL is FLASHING: stop driving immediately. Catalyst damage occurs within minutes.
Engine at operating temperature for accurate diagnosis
Record all stored DTCs — any P030X alongside P0300 reveals worst cylinder

procedure_steps.description: SEVERITY CHECK FIRST: Is MIL flashing or steady? FLASHING = unburned fuel entering catalyst, stop engine. STEADY = less urgent but still diagnose before damage. Note any P030X codes — the specific cylinder is your starting point.
Read freeze frame: Mode $02. Note: RPM when fault set (idle misfire vs load misfire), engine load, coolant temp, fuel trims. Cold-start misfire = different cause than warm-idle or WOT misfire.

Read per-cylinder misfire counts if available: Mode $01 PID $4C (cyl 1) through $53 (cyl 8). Cylinder(s) with highest count = start there. If all cylinders equal misfire counts = true random (fuel/air/timing issue).

IGNITION ISOLATION (COP engines): Swap coil from suspect cylinder with adjacent known-good cylinder. If misfire MOVES with the coil = coil is bad. If misfire STAYS on same cylinder = not the coil (spark plug or injector or compression).

SPARK PLUG CHECK: Remove spark plug from suspect cylinder. Look for: fouled (black/oily = rich/oil burning), cracked ceramic, gap wear (>0.060 inch on most). Replace plugs if >60k miles — random misfire from worn plugs is the #1 cause.

FUEL TRIM CHECK with misfire: Read STFT/LTFT (Mode $01 PID $06/$07). If lean trims + misfire = lean misfire (vacuum leak, MAF, fuel pressure). Rich trims + misfire = flooding (injector stuck open, O2 sensor). Neutral trims + misfire = ignition or mechanical.

COMPRESSION TEST (if ignition/fuel ruled out): Perform compression test on suspect cylinder(s). Normal: 140-200 psi depending on engine. Cylinder 20+ psi below others or below 120 psi = mechanical issue (rings, valves, head gasket).

EGR/EVAP CROSS-CHECK: P0300 with lean trims and EVAP monitor not ready = stuck-open purge valve flooding intake at idle (causes random rich misfire paradox). Command purge valve closed via actuator test — if misfire resolves: purge valve issue.
After repair: clear all misfire codes. Drive cycle: idle 5 min, 0-60 acceleration 3x, highway cruise 10 min. Read misfire counters — all should be zero or near-zero. No P0300 return. MIL steady off.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: 2GR V6: coil failures on cylinders 1/3/5 (front bank) more common. A25A: carbon on intake valves causes lean misfire at idle. Check for P0011/P0012 VVT codes alongside P0300 — cam timing fault causes mechanical misfire.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: K/J/R series: spark plug fouling from oil consumption (valve stem seals on high-mileage). Check oil level and plug color. VTEC system fault can cause load-based misfire — check VTEC oil pressure.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: VQ35DE: known coil-on-plug failures. IPDM-ER fuel pump relay issues cause fuel pressure misfire. Check all 6 coils — swap test pairs.
oem_notes.Ford: EcoBoost 3.5T: P0300 often from ignition coils (Motorcraft coils fail at 60k). 5.0L: IMRC runner control failures can cause load-based P0300. Check for P0022 alongside misfire — cam phaser.
oem_notes.GM: 5.3L L83/L84: AFM lifter collapse causes P0300 + P030X on deactivation cylinders (1/4/6/7). AFM pattern = misfire only at light throttle (deactivation mode). Disable AFM to confirm.
oem_notes.BMW: N20/N26: timing chain stretch at high mileage causes P0300 at startup. Check for P0016/P0017. N54/N55: injector failures cause single-cylinder misfire. HPFP low pressure = global misfire.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: EA888: P0300 + lean trims = PCV diaphragm rupture (most common). EA888 Gen1/2: carbon on intake valves causes cold misfire that clears when warm.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Hemi 5.7: MDS solenoid failure causes P0300 at highway speeds (deactivation misfire). Pentastar 3.6L: cam follower failures on high-mileage causing random valve timing misfire.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Theta II 2.0T/2.4T: GDI carbon on intake valves = cold start random misfire. Lambda 3.3T: coil failures. Check TSBs for engine failure history (Theta II engine seizure recall).
